items.title: ACT Greens leader Shane Rattenbury quitting politics

children.content: The leader of the ACT Greens and former attorney-general Shane Rattenbury has stepped down from his position and says he is quitting politics.

gallery.altText: A man in a charcoal suit and green tie stands behind two microphone stands speaking seriously.

thumbnail.altText: A man in a charcoal suit and green tie stands behind two microphone stands speaking seriously.

avatar.alt: Niki Burnside is a journalist and digital producer based in Canberra.

items.title: The skis being made from three types of Australian-grown timber

children.content: A couple in the NSW Snowy Mountains makes skis entirely from Australian-grown timber, though manufacturing for the snow industry in a "summer country" comes with its share of challenges.

image.altText: A man and a woman holding skis in a workshop setting, with a small baby.

items.title: This type of croquet is a far cry from the Alice in Wonderland version

children.content: Golf croquet helps isolated elderly people reconnect with others through social competition, morning tea and laughs.

image.altText: Elderly woman sitting on a stool in garden holding a purple croquet mallet

items.title: 'Feel it when I breathe': Canberra's first dedicated vaping support program opens

children.content: The percentage of young people who have tried a vape has almost tripled since 2018, but Canberra's first dedicated vaping support program says young people are taking it upon themselves to overcome addiction.

gallery.altText: A man with short dark hair sits on an outdoor bench vaping.

thumbnail.altText: A man with short dark hair sits on an outdoor bench vaping.

items.title: Arrest warrant issued in legal battle for $27k in unpaid long service leave

children.content: It's now been over three years since two women left their jobs at a small business. Two courts later, they want their combined $27,000 in long service leave paid out to them, and say they won't give up until they get it.

image.altText: two women sitting at a dining table looking through paperwork

items.title: Two men found guilty of manslaughter over 1999 death of Irma Palasics

children.content: Two Melbourne men are found guilty of the manslaughter of Canberra woman Irma Palasics during a violent home invasion in 1999.

image.altText: Irma Palasics smiles at the camera, she has short curly hair and wears a pink knit vest.

items.title: New scooters and e-bikes have arrived in Canberra. Here's what that means

children.content: With Neuron's orange scooters having left Canberra, Lime has introduced a fleet of green scooters and e-bikes across the capital in partnership with the ACT government.

gallery.altText: A person rides a green Lime bike near a fountain in an urban space.

thumbnail.altText: A person rides a green Lime bike near a fountain in an urban space.

items.title: The world is on the cusp of an 'era of extreme scarcity'. Enter Vladimir Putin

children.content: With fears hunger could hit record levels, Vladimir Putin and Russia are poised to capitalise — financially and diplomatically.

image.altText: Vladimir Putin sits at a desk with a map in front of him in the Kremlin

items.title: Price of groceries to increase within weeks due to war in Iran

children.content: Farmers ask for a 30-cent-per-litre price rise for milk as fuel and fertiliser prices surge.

image.altText: A shopping trolley full of essentials items like fruit, water, and eggs

items.title: 'Hard to break the habit': Why free public transport isn't enough to shift from cars

children.content: Free public transport is a popular policy, but researchers say it's not enough to convince drivers to leave the car at home.

image.altText: A train on a city railway.

items.title: Grieving mother of Belgian backpacker warns hikers not to trust phone maps

children.content: Backpacker Celine Cremer set off on a short walk with only a phone to guide her — and vanished. More than two years later, her family has an urgent message for other travellers.

items.title: Chalmers to hold talks with states as he flags further NDIS 'savings'

children.content: Treasurer Jim Chalmers is flagging more "savings" to the National Disability Insurance Scheme as the government seeks to curb growth to about 5 per cent. Follow live.

image.altText: Men in suits speak at a press conference

items.title: Two-thirds of Australians leave private information open to cybercriminals

children.content: New research suggests Australians are leaving themselves vulnerable to cybercrime through their public social media accounts.

image.altText: A pair of hands typing on a laptop

items.title: RSL denies services club's Anzac Day two-up request

children.content: An ex-services organisation wants changes to Queensland legislation that allows the local RSL branch to reject its application to play two-up on Anzac Day.

items.title: ASX flat on rising Hormuz tension

children.content: The ASX has opened flat on rising Hormuz tensions as oil prices rebound from Friday's slump. Follow the day's events and insights from our business reporters on the ABC News live markets blog.

image.altText: A stock trader looks down at a tablet as he works on the floor of a busy stock exchange while surrounded by screens.

items.title: Blood donation eligibility expands for gay and bisexual men, transgender people

children.content: From today, Australian Red Cross Lifeblood will use gender-neutral assessments for blood and platelets donations.

items.title: What it's like creating a concert shaped by the audience's most-voted pieces

children.content: The ABC Classic 100 in Concert brings Australia's most popular classical music, as chosen by audiences, from the radio to the stage.

image.altText: Benjamin Northey conducts the Melbourne Symphony in a tuxedo. His hair is flying in the air and his arms gesture dramatically.

items.title: Profits shrivel as yellow tail winemaker posts $5.5 million loss

children.content: The maker of one of Australia's best known wine brands, yellow tail, says rising costs and global factors such as tariffs are behind the sharp drop in profits.

children.content: Farmers in the midst of harvest in South Australia are having to get creative to help make ends meet.

image.altText: A fair-skinned woman in a red floral dress holds a cupcake on a glass plate in a kitchen.

items.title: US domestic violence shooting leaves eight children dead

children.content: The victims, who were shot at two homes in the US state of Louisiana, ranged in age from one to about 14 years old.

image.altText: Shreveport shooting

items.title: Thrills and spills: How ex-NRL star Lomax fared in rugby milestone moment

children.content: Even if Zac Lomax's starting debut for the Western Force had been disastrous, it would still deserve to be considered a success.

items.title: Fire engulfs school building ahead of students' return from holidays

items.title: Thousands displaced as fire destroys homes on Malaysia's Borneo island

children.content: Thousands of people are displaced after a fire destroys about 1,000 homes in a coastal village in Malaysia's Sabah state, the fire department says.

image.altText: piles of rubble and sheets of corrugated iron.

items.title: The Queensland region where locals avoid their own hospitals

children.content: In Queensland's Lockyer Valley, new figures suggest a third of the region's 41 hospital beds are not regularly available for new patients.

gallery.altText: A young girl in a hospital bed with a cast on her arm.

thumbnail.altText: A young girl in a hospital bed with a cast on her arm.

items.title: Push to tax gas companies 'ripping up our oceans' as Gorgon plan approved

children.content: Natural gas taxes are back in the fray after the expansion of Chevron's Gorgon project off WA's north-west coast received environmental approval amid a Senate inquiry into how resources are taxed.

image.altText: A drone image of Chevron's Gorgon project on Barrow Island.

items.title: Report reveals nation's best and worst attempts to tackle illicit tobacco

children.content: Smoking is one of the leading causes of death in the Northern Territory. Now, the jurisdiction has been ranked the weakest in the country for how it tackles cheap, illicit tobacco.

image.altText: A woman with shoulder-length blonde hair, inspecting the packets on cigarettes in her hands.

items.title: Police release footage of divers' rescue after more than 20 hours at sea

children.content: The two divers were spotted by a jet more than 14 nautical miles from where they went missing on Saturday morning.

image.altText: A person descends from a helicopter at sea above a boat

items.title: Man faces Melbourne court after pedestrian death at comic convention

children.content: Awer Dau, 33, faces the Melbourne Magistrates' Court on Sunday evening, charged with culpable driving causing death. He allegedly hit a 20-year-old man while driving outside the Supanova Comic Con and Gaming convention on Saturday.

image.altText: Bunches of flowers against a fence.

items.title: Netball star calls for pay increase after new broadcast deal

children.content: Diamonds and Melbourne Vixens star Jo Weston calls for a pay increase after a new free-to-air broadcast deal.

items.title: Who was C Gordon? NSW community tries to solve WWI soldier mystery

children.content: The names etched into the Araluen War Memorial are read aloud every Anzac Day, but one digger from World War I remains a mystery.

items.title: Schools deemed safe after WA's biggest lithium-ion battery fire

children.content: Schools in Perth's south-east have been deemed safe for students to attend after WA's biggest lithium-ion battery fire sparked a HAZMAT warning in Maddington, which is still in place.

image.altText: Flames can be seen billowing from a building in an industrial area.

items.title: Tasmania's opposition has declared it's 'back', but the work isn't done yet

children.content: The Labor party's first state conference in seven years reunited old friends and potentially a few "frenemies", but with issues like greyhound racing absent, drama was largely kept at bay.
